zh.lifeSummary: 當Helen Quincy於1222年12月14日在英國哈普爾維古姆受洗時，她的父親Roger de Quincy 2nd Earl of Winchester27歲，母親Helen de Galloway22歲。她於1242年與Alan la Zouche I I在英國英格蘭哈普爾維古姆結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1296年8月27日在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾萊希斯特享年73歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾。

nameMeanings.name: Quincy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of several places in France deriving their names from the Gallo-Roman personal name Quintus, meaning ‘fifth(-born)’ + the locative suffix -acum. The earliest bearer of the name in England, that of Saer de Quincy, ancestor of the earls of Winchester, was from Cuinchy in Pas-de-Calais, but the name may have been reinforced by immigrants from Quincy-sous-Sénard in Seine-et-Oise or Quincy-Voisins in Seine-et-Marne. A family of the name de Quincy held the title Earl of Winchester 1207–65. Descendants of this family later bore the name Quincey up to the time of the English essayist Thomas De Quincey (1785–1859), who changed his name from Quincey to De Quincey in 1796.

History: The American Quincy family were established in MA by Edmund Quincy in 1633. Fifth in descent was Josiah Quincy (1744–75), a leading patriot, who was sent to England to argue the colonists’ case in 1774. His son Josiah (1772–1864) was a powerful opponent of slavery, president of Harvard, and mayor of Boston, a post also held by several of his descendants. The traditional pronunciation is “Quinzy”.

nameMeanings.name: Helen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: vernacular form of the name (Greek Hēlēnē) borne in classical legend by a famous beauty, wife of Menelaus, whose seizure by the Trojan prince Paris sparked off the Trojan War. Her name is of uncertain origin; it may be connected with a word meaning ‘ray’ or ‘sunbeam’ compare Greek hēlios ‘sun’. It has sometimes been taken as connected with the Greek word meaning ‘Greek’, Hellēn, but this is doubtful. In the early Christian period the name was borne by the mother of the Emperor Constantine, who is now usually known by the Latin version of her name, Helena. She is credited with having found the True Cross in Jerusalem. She was born in about 248 , probably in Bithynia. However, in medieval England it was believed that she had been born in Britain, which greatly increased the popularity of the name there.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Helen Quincy was christened on 14 December 1222, in Winchester, Hampshire, England, her father, Roger de Quincy 2nd Earl of Winchester, was 27 and her mother, Helen de Galloway, was 22. She married Alan la Zouche I I in 1242, in Winchester, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 27 August 1296, in Leicester, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 73, and was buried in Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Helen Quincy a été baptisée le 14 décembre 1222, à Winchester, Hampshire, Angleterre, son père, Roger de Quincy 2nd Earl of Winchester, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Helen de Galloway, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé Alan la Zouche I I en 1242, à Winchester, Hampshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 27 août 1296, à Leicester, Leicestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 73 ans, et a été enterrée à Leicestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.lifeSummary: Helen Quincy(이)가 1222년 12월 14일 영국 함프실르 윈체스터에서 침례를 받았을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Roger de Quincy 2nd Earl of Winchester(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Helen de Galloway(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1242년 영국 잉글랜드 함프실르 윈체스터에서 Alan la Zouche I I(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1296년 8월 27일 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르 레이스스터에서 73세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르에 묻혔다.

pt.14 December 1222–27 August 1296: 14 de dezembro de 1222–27 de agosto de 1296
pt.Black Torrington, Devon, England: Black Torrington, Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.Ashby, Leicestershire, England: Ashby de la Zouch, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.Ashby-de-la-Zouch Castle, Leicestershire: Ashby de la Zouch, Leicestershire, Inglaterra

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Helen Quincy foi batizada em 14 de dezembro de 1222, em Winchester, Hampshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Roger de Quincy 2nd Earl of Winchester, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Helen de Galloway, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com Alan la Zouche I I em 1242, em Winchester, Hampshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 27 de agosto de 1296, em Leicester, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 73 anos, e foi sepultada em Leicestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Helen Quincy была крещена 14 декабря 1222 г. в местности винчестир, хампшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Roger de Quincy 2nd Earl of Winchester было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Helen de Galloway, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Alan la Zouche I I в 1242 г. в местности винчестир, хампшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Она умерла 27 августа 1296 г. в местности лисестир, лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 73 лет и была похоронена в местности лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Helen Quincy se bautizó el 14 de diciembre de 1222, en Winchester, Hampshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Roger de Quincy 2nd Earl of Winchester, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Helen de Galloway, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Alan la Zouche I I en 1242, en Winchester, Hampshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 27 de agosto de 1296, en Leicester, Leicestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 73 años, y fue sepultada en Leicestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
